overview.tagline: A tight-knit community along the Hackensack River
overview.description: Bogota is a compact, working-class borough situated along the west bank of the Hackensack River in Bergen County. With just under one square mile of land, Bogota packs a strong sense of community into its small footprint. The borough features affordable housing, a friendly downtown along Queen Anne Road, and easy access to Route 80 and major NJ Transit bus routes. Its diverse population and community-oriented character make it a welcoming place for families and newcomers.
highlights: Compact, walkable community with small-town character
Affordable housing by Bergen County standards
Strong community spirit with active volunteer organizations
Convenient access to Route 80 and NYC bus routes

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. The borough participates in Bergen County assessment programs.
realEstate.recentTrends: Bogota has seen healthy appreciation as buyers seek affordable entry points to Bergen County. The compact size and community feel attract young families and first-time homebuyers. Two-family homes are common and popular with investors.
neighborhoodNotes: Queen Anne Road corridor is the main commercial street with mixed-use properties
Riverfront properties along the Hackensack offer water views
The residential core features modest single-family and two-family homes on small lots
Areas near Olsen Park are popular with families

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the borough's contracted hauler.
details: Collection days are Monday and Thursday
Trash must be curbside by 6:00 AM in sealed containers
Maximum weight per container is 50 lbs
Bulk pickup available by appointment

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Recycling is collected weekly through the Bergen County Utilities Authority program.
details: Commingled recyclables in designated bins
Paper and cardboard collected on alternating weeks
Check borough website for recycling calendar
Electronics and hazardous waste drop-off events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is provided by Veolia Water. Sewer service is managed by the borough with treatment through BCUA.
details: Water bills issued quarterly
Sewer charges appear on property tax bills
Report water emergencies to DPW
Water quality reports available annually

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Construction Department handles all building permits and inspections.
details: Permits required for all structural, electrical, and plumbing work
Zoning Board reviews variance applications
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction and renovations
Applications available at Borough Hall

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: On-street parking is regulated throughout the borough with some municipal lot parking available.
details: Metered parking along Queen Anne Road commercial district
Residential parking permits may be required in certain areas
Overnight street parking restricted
Snow emergency parking rules strictly enforced

additionalServices.title: Yard Waste Collection
additionalServices.description: Seasonal yard waste pickup for leaves, grass clippings, and brush.
details: Fall leaf collection October through December
Grass and brush must be in biodegradable bags or bundles
No mixing yard waste with regular trash

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Noise regulations protect the residential character of this compact borough.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM weekdays
Saturday construction 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM only
No construction on Sundays or holidays
Excessive noise complaints subject to municipal court action

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties to borough standards.
keyRules: Lawns must be kept trimmed and orderly
Sidewalks must be maintained and cleared of snow within 24 hours
No storage of inoperable vehicles on residential properties
Fences must be maintained in good repair

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: All dogs must be licensed and controlled.
keyRules: Dog licenses required annually from Borough Clerk
Dogs must be leashed at all times when off property
Owners must immediately clean up pet waste
Rabies vaccination required

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking is regulated given the borough's compact layout.
keyRules: No overnight street parking 2:00 AM to 6:00 AM
No parking during snow emergencies
Alternate side parking enforced for street cleaning
Violations result in ticketing and potential towing

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must be registered and comply with housing codes.
keyRules: All rental units must be registered with the borough
Inspections required at tenant turnover
Certificate of Habitability required
Landlords responsible for code compliance

other.title: Flood Zone Regulations
other.summary: Parts of Bogota along the Hackensack River are in flood zones with special building requirements.
keyRules: Properties in flood zones must carry flood insurance
New construction and substantial improvements must meet elevation requirements
Fill and grading in flood zones require permits
Borough participates in FEMA's National Flood Insurance Program
other.source: Borough of Bogota Flood Damage Prevention Ordinance

publicSchools.name: Bogota Junior/Senior High School
publicSchools.notes: Combined junior and senior high school serving the borough's older students

privateSchools.name: St. Joseph School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school affiliated with St. Joseph Parish

whyMoveHere: One of the most affordable towns in Bergen County
Easy highway access to Route 80 and the GWB
Good starter-home market for first-time buyers

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit bus to Port Authority or PATH
commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit buses run along River Road and Route 46 providing service to the Port Authority Bus Terminal. Driving to the GWB takes about 20 minutes. Some commuters drive to Teterboro or Anderson Street stations for rail connections.

costOfLiving.summary: Bogota is one of Bergen County's most affordable communities, making it attractive for first-time buyers and young families.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Near or slightly below the New Jersey average for housing, but property taxes are comparable
costOfLiving.vsNationalAverage: Above the national average, consistent with the northern NJ region

cons: High property tax rates despite lower home values
Small school district with limited program variety
Compact lots with limited yard space

restaurants.name: Sagres Bar & Grill
restaurants.description: Portuguese and Spanish cuisine with a casual, neighborhood atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Queen Anne Pizza
restaurants.description: Classic New Jersey pizzeria serving pies, heroes, and Italian favorites.

restaurants.name: Sabor Latino
restaurants.description: Authentic Latin American home cooking popular with locals.

parks.name: Olsen Park
parks.description: Main borough park with playgrounds, athletic fields, basketball courts, and picnic areas.
parks.address: W Fort Lee Rd, Bogota, NJ 07603

parks.name: Riverside Park
parks.description: Small waterfront park along the Hackensack River with benches and river views.

parks.name: Bogota Recreation Fields
parks.description: Athletic complex with fields for soccer, baseball, and community sports leagues.

events.name: Bogota Day
events.description: Annual community celebration with food vendors, live entertainment, games, and activities in Olsen Park.

events.name: Holiday Parade
events.description: Annual holiday parade through the borough featuring local organizations, school bands, and Santa Claus.

attractions.name: Bogota Public Library
attractions.description: Community library offering programs, book clubs, and children's activities.

attractions.name: Hackensack Riverfront
attractions.description: Scenic riverfront offering views, fishing, and peaceful walks along the Hackensack River.

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the second and fourth Thursday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Bogota Borough Hall, 375 Larch Ave, Bogota, NJ 07603
